steps.title: Find DC using the 30-60-90 triangle BDC
steps.content:
Triangle $BDC$ has a right angle at $D$ because $BD \perp AC$, angle $BCD = 60^{\circ}$, and angle $DBC = 30^{\circ}$. This is a 30-60-90 right triangle.

In a 30-60-90 triangle, the sides opposite $30^{\circ}$, $60^{\circ}$, and $90^{\circ}$ are in the ratio $1: \sqrt{3}: 2$.

With $BC = 12$ as the hypotenuse, we have:
$$DC = 12 \cdot \frac{1}{2} = 6$$
$$BD = 12 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = 6\sqrt{3}$$

steps.title: Find AD using the 30-60-90 triangle ABD
steps.content:
Triangle $ABD$ has a right angle at $D$, angle $ABD = 30^{\circ}$, and angle $BAD = 60^{\circ}$. This is also a 30-60-90 triangle.

Using the same side ratio, with $BD = 6\sqrt{3}$ as the side opposite $60^{\circ}$:
$$AD = \frac{6\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}} = 6$$
That is the correct answer.
